response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems engineer with a broad portfolio, but he lacks the core technical qualifications for this role. His C++ exposure is limited to a six-month position over a decade ago and a bachelor thesis project; there is no Rust experience at all. While he has genuine geospatial experience through GISCollective and Green Map, that work is oriented around event-driven data collection and web GIS for NGOs — not routing tiles, navigation pipelines, or real-time traffic integration. His high-performance systems work at Dunnhumby used DLang, not C++ or Rust, and was focused on ad-tech HTTP throughput rather than geospatial or navigation domains. The gap between his current skill set and the role's primary requirements is too wide to bridge without a significant re-tooling investment.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective and Green Map, including a FOSS4G conference talk on open geo-data platforms
Demonstrated ability to build high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems (async I/O, fibers, caching at Dunnhumby handling millions of requests/second)
Broad systems breadth across Linux, distributed infrastructure, CI/CD, and multiple paradigms, suggesting adaptability when given ramp-up time
concerns: C++ proficiency is effectively absent at a professional level — last meaningful use was a 6-month role in 2014–2015; no Rust experience whatsoever
No experience with routing tiles, navigation guidance systems, or map data pipelines, which are the core deliverables of the role
Primary career trajectory is firmly in web/TypeScript/Node.js full-stack work; the resume shows no movement toward systems programming in recent years
